symptoms.symptom_name: Annular scaly advancing-border plaques with central clearing, expanding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Annular plaques with a raised scaly advancing border and central clearing are characteristic of tinea corporis.
Erythema migrans is smooth, non-scaly and usually not itchy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Topical steroid use accelerating spread (tinea incognito)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Steroid-driven spread with loss of the typical morphology indicates tinea incognito.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intense pruritus, worse at night and with sweating

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marked itch favours dermatophytosis over the typically asymptomatic erythema migrans.

symptoms.symptom_name: Secondary bacterial infection (hot/hard swelling, thick pus, fever)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative screen keeps this a benign topical-treatment case; a positive screen would upgrade urgency and add antibiotics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Clinical diagnosis (± skin scraping / KOH microscopy if uncertain)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Tinea corporis is usually a clinical diagnosis; scraping confirms it when unclear, especially when steroid-modified.
Not indicated for uncomplicated localised tinea corporis with no systemic features.
